macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna: Eishes Achiv She'lo Hayah Be'olamo and the Role of Zika

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and transition from previous discussion of zika
Setup of the case: Reuven, Shimon, and Levi with Rachel as the key woman
The central question: when did Shimon perform yibum with Rachel

macro_segments.title: The Mishna's Two Cases: Before and After Yibum

micro_segments.title: First case (Reisha): Levi born before Shimon performs yibum with Rachel
Second case (Seifa): Yibum performed before Levi is born; maamar without yibum
Dispute between Tanna Kamma and Rabbi Shimon on status change

macro_segments.title: Rav Oshaya's Theory: Rabbi Shimon Disagrees Even in the Reisha

micro_segments.title: Rav Oshaya's argument that the Reisha is superfluous according to the Rabbis
Why the Reisha must represent Rabbi Shimon's unique position
Visual representation of the two cases on the board
Discussion of Rachel's status before and after Shimon's marriage

macro_segments.title: Zika and Its Power: The Mechanism Behind Rabbi Shimon's Position

micro_segments.title: Gemara's question: how can there be eishes achiv she'lo hayah be'olamo according to Rabbi Shimon
Answer: the case of two brothers without yibum having occurred

macro_segments.title: Zika and Its Power: The Mechanism Behind Rabbi Shimon's Position (continued)

micro_segments.title: Explanation of zika as making the woman like the brother's wife (keknusa damya)

macro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's Challenge: Double Zika and the Limits of Zika's Power

micro_segments.title: Rav Yosef's objection: ma'amar plus zika may not make her fully like a wife
Case of three brothers with two dying: Rachel and Sarah's dual status
Explanation of why Levi cannot do yibum with both women
Levi must do chalitza with both because of dual zika problem
Rabbi Shimon's position: he denies the dual zika problem and allows choice of yibum or chalitza

macro_segments.title: Gemara's Refinement: Zika Status is Doubtful, Not Dual

micro_segments.title: Abaye's suggestion: zika may work with one brother but not with two
Gemara's investigation of whether zika plus maamar creates firm marital status
Conclusion: Rabbi Shimon sees a safek (doubt) rather than definitive dual status

macro_segments.title: The Baraita Evidence: Rabbi Shimon's Own Words on Birth Timing

micro_segments.title: Citation of baraita: birth before nisuin means no chalitza or yibum
Baraita's second case: nisuin before birth means chalitza or yibum required
Gemara's difficulty: why mention two brothers if only birth timing matters
Attempted resolution: the baraita assumes two brothers (Shimon and Issachar)
Final rejection: Gemara concludes the baraita only cares about birth timing, not brother count

macro_segments.title: Competing Wives Problem: Sisters and Relatives Who Cannot Marry Together

micro_segments.title: Setup: two brothers married to sisters (or mother-daughter, etc.) who both die
Problem: Levi cannot do yibum with both wives or with one and ignore the other
Solution required: chalitza with both women
Rabbi Shimon's position: both women are exempt (patur)
Rav Amram's reading: only the second wife is exempt

macro_segments.title: Rava's Problematic Reinterpretation and Its Rejection

micro_segments.title: Rava's theory: the brothers are married to multiple pairs of wives
Gemara's rejection: the text says 'or' not 'and'
Further objection: Rabbi Shimon should say all four are exempt if Rava is right

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Shimon's True Position: The Tzarot Principle and Simultaneous Death

micro_segments.title: Baraita: when wives are tzarot (competing), neither can be taken
Rav Ashi's resolution: both women exempt only if the brothers died simultaneously
Rabbi Shimon holds like Rabbi Yossi HaGlili: simultaneous events are possible
Sequential death: Rabbi Shimon would follow the first husband's wife

macro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Conclusion: Rabbi Shimon's Dispute Limited to Yibum Before Birth

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa: Rabbi Shimon disputes only when yibum occurred before Levi's birth
Rav Papa: Rabbi Shimon does not dispute when Levi born before yibum
Summary: rejection of Rav Oshaya's reading; accepting Rav Papa's pshat

topical_tags.term: Yibum / Yibum before vs after Levi's birth
